key_insight: SILAGE is a variance-reduced optimizer for nested finite sums that removes costly full-data gradient refreshes while using only O(n) memory by leveraging the problem’s block structure.
engineering_relevance: This makes large-scale, centralized learning on partitioned or out-of-core datasets more practical when memory and communication overhead are bottlenecks.
method: Uses a nested double-sum formulation to update with at most one local group gradient per iteration.
Avoids periodic global full-gradient computations over all nm samples.
Tracks only O(n) control information, with complexity adapting to cross-group (δ1) and within-group (δ2) data similarity.
evidence_signal: The abstract claims improved state-of-the-art complexity bounds and a tighter analysis that reflects data geometry rather than worst-case Lipschitz constants.
watch_item: Performance depends on how well the block structure and heterogeneity parameters δ1 and δ2 match the real dataset; poor grouping may reduce the gains.
paper_title: SILAGE: Memory-Efficient, Full-Gradient-Free Nonconvex Optimization for Nested Finite Sums
